title: Mildly Smoked Zhejiang Kippers Dumplings
description: A Chinese-inspired post-workout dish featuring kippers and tender dumplings for a high-protein meal. Perfect for a satisfying snack or light dinner after a workout.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine flour, baking powder, sea salt, and granulated sugar.
Add the egg and soy sauce to the bowl and mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5-7 minutes until it's smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into 12 equal pieces and roll each piece into a ball.
Flatten each ball into a disk using a rolling pin.
Place a tablespoon of water in the center of each disk and top with a kipper fillet.
Brush the edges of each disk with egg and fold the dough over the filling to form a half-circle shape.
Press the edges of each dumpling to seal them tightly.
Heat sesame oil in a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat.
Add the dumplings to the skillet and cook for 2-3 minutes on each side until they're golden brown.
Transfer the dumplings to a steamer basket and steam them for 5 minutes.
Serve the dumplings hot with a sprinkle of scallions and mildly smoked salt.
